last_item: Confirm CSRF protection on every state-changing endpoint
last_result: done — added TestEveryStateChangingEndpointRejectsCSRF, which chi.Walks the real route table and asserts every unsafe-method route rejects a forged cross-origin cookie request (409/CodeSafety), except a documented exempt set (login/logout) that self-checks against registered routes so a new mutating route outside requireAuth fails the test; reviewer found no blocking issues; all gates green; test-only change
notes: CSRF coverage proven across the whole route table (requireAuth is the central gate; new test is a regression guard for routes added outside it). Remaining band-1 items: DB-level read-only role enforcement (test a write through the read-only role fails at Postgres), secrets-at-rest (DB file 0600 + log-scrubbing test), and login brute-force lockout (lockout exists in internal/auth — verify the throttle is end-to-end tested via the login handler).
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
